name: Spicy Szechwan Chicken with Cashew Nuts-Kung
recipeIngredient: 3 Whole chicken breasts, skin removed, boned, and cut into 1-inch squares
1 inch piece of fresh ginger, root peeled and minced
1/2 to 1 ts Indonesian Hot Pepper Paste (see recipe)
recipeInstructions:
1. Coat chicken with cornstarch in a bag and shake until well coated. Beat egg white in a bowl, add chicken, and stir. Refrigerate for 30 minutes.
2. Heat oil in a wok over medium heat. When it develops a slight haze (do not let it smoke), fry cashew nuts until light golden brown. Drain over the wok with a slotted spoon and set aside on a paper plate.
3. Reheat oil over medium-high heat, then stir-fry chicken pieces until firm and light golden. Drain over the wok and set aside on paper plates.
4. In the same oil, fry ginger and onions for 1 minute, stirring constantly. Stir in seasonings except sesame oil and sugar.
5. Add chicken pieces to the wok again and stir-fry for 1 minute.
6. Add sesame oil and sugar, then cashews. Transfer to a platter and serve.
Indonesian Hot Pepper Paste:
30 dried red chili peppers, stemmed (remove seeds for milder version)
Hot water, sufficient to cover the peppers
White vinegar, sufficient to make a paste
Salt to taste
Combine peppers in a bowl with hot water; let soak for 10-15 minutes. Drain well and place peppers and 4 tablespoons vinegar in a blender. Blend into a rough paste, adding as much vinegar as necessary to achieve desired consistency and allow blades to turn easily. Add salt and mix well. Transfer mixture to a sterilized jar and cap tightly. Refrigerate. Yield: about 1/2 cup.
